title: Wages Are Falling. Wealth Is Surging. No Wonder Americans Are Unhappy.
summary: As Elon Musk became the world’s first trillionaire, workers are facing higher prices and fears of A.I.-driven job losses.

title: SpaceX’s Unlikely Journey From Far-Out Idea to $2 Trillion Juggernaut
summary: Elon Musk said he had initially given SpaceX less than a 10 percent chance of succeeding. His rocket company has come a long way.

title: Mega I.P.O. Frenzy Could Be a Harbinger of a Stock Bubble
summary: Rampant enthusiasm is buoying tech shares to levels that defy gravity. Invest with caution, our columnist says.

title: Kennedy Center Begins Removing Trump’s Name From Facade
summary: The arts institution followed a judge’s order to take down President Trump’s name after seeking a 12-hour extension, attributing the delay to thunderstorms.

title: Judge Blocks National Parks From Removing ‘Negative’ Signs and Depictions of Slavery
summary: The ruling accused the Trump administration of engaging in censorship by taking down materials at parks across the country.

title: A Tren de Aragua Leader Is Killed in a Joint Strike, U.S. and Venezuela Say
summary: A strike this week in Venezuela killed a gang leader known as Niño Guerrero who was wanted in the United States, officials in both countries said.

title: Anthropic Blocks Foreigners From Using Mythos and Fable AI
summary: The company said on Friday night that the federal government had ordered limits on its Mythos and Fable 5 A.I. systems, citing national security concerns.

title: With a Deal Seemingly Close, the U.S. Faces an Iran More Willing to Withstand Pressure
summary: The war has produced regime change, but Iran’s new leaders are more willing to take risks and believe they have already absorbed the worst that America and Israel can deliver.

title: Despite US Help, Little Oil Has Gone Through Strait of Hormuz
summary: President Trump said more than 200 commercial vessels had safely traveled through the strait. That’s still far fewer than before the start of the war.

title: What to Know About the Ebola Outbreak
summary: Aid agencies are racing to help health workers in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The virus is known to have killed at least 140 people, but the true toll may be far higher.

title: New Yorkers Are Going on the Road With the Knicks for Game 5
summary: One resale site said that more than 48 percent of its tickets to the game in San Antonio had been sold to people from New York and New Jersey.

title: Has There Ever Been a Crazier Sports Moment for New York? Actually, Yes.
summary: In the spring of 1994, the World Cup arrived, the Knicks were great and so were the Rangers. And in the middle of it all, an infamous White Bronco chase.

title: Seth Rogen Knows the Secret to Marriage — and Being Rich in Hollywood
summary: After three decades in the industry, the actor-writer-director-producer has figured a lot out.

title: David Hockney and the Bliss of Not Standing Still
summary: “As important as the boys and the pools and the light,” a memoirist writes, “the most important thing was becoming the driving.” It would inspire an obsession with moving focus into the future.

title: Jeffrey Epstein Haunts the White House
summary: A sleazy situation that Trump can’t wish away.

title: The Spectacle of Trump at 80
summary: An octogenarian president for a 250-year-old republic.

title: Unfinished Business
summary: We like it when projects are completed, but what if we could abide comfortably in the toiling and striving it takes to get them there?

title: Justice Dept. Clears Way for Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
summary: The $111 billion deal would unite two major movie studios and put CNN under the same roof as CBS News.
